name: Detroit Hispanic Development Corporation
description_short: Detroit Hispanic Development Corporation is a non-profit community center in Southwest Detroit offering free HUD-approved housing counseling, financial literacy, youth programs, and family services to 5,000+ residents annually.
description_long:
Detroit Hispanic Development Corporation (DHDC) was founded in 1997 by Angela Reyes in response to gang violence and youth crime in Southwest Detroit. Starting in a living room with support from community leaders including Rev. Robert Duggan, local police, and Hispanic business owners, DHDC grew into a comprehensive community development organization. In 2001, DHDC relocated to a 28,000-square-foot facility at 1211 Trumbull Street in Corktown, transformed from a former manufacturing building into a multi-service hub. The organization is deeply rooted in Southwest Detroit's Latino culture and community needs.

DHDC offers a wide range of free and low-cost programs across three main areas: youth and family programs, adult education and workforce development, and financial services. Youth services include the Urban Arts Academy, Best Buy Teen Tech Center, Summer Youth Program, Youth Ambassador Scholarship Program, and Robotics & Engineering Center of Detroit. Family services encompass HUD Housing Counseling, Financial Literacy classes, Family Education, Padres Unidos, Parent Mentor Program, and the Parent Institute. For adults, DHDC provides Case Management, the Freedom Ink Tattoo-Removal Program, and Entrepreneurship & Wealth Building training.

DHDC distinguishes itself through its deep community roots, culturally-responsive programming, and focus on historically underserved populations including Spanish-speaking families and at-risk youth. The organization was born from addressing gang violence through employment and education, demonstrating a commitment to transforming lives through practical opportunity creation. Their track record includes removing 800+ tattoos through the Freedom Ink program, reaching 50,000 individuals through outreach, and distributing over $1 million in emergency financial assistance during COVID-19.

DHDC is genuinely a non-profit community organization with no profit motive, making it a reliable resource for free financial counseling and educational services. However, consumers should note that DHDC's primary service area is Southwest Detroit and surrounding communities, so access may be limited for those outside this region. The organization appears to prioritize family and youth services alongside financial help, rather than specializing exclusively in credit or debt counseling like some dedicated financial counseling agencies.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselors on staff providing free counseling
Free financial literacy classes for families and adults
Serves 5,000+ people annually with no-cost or low-cost programming
Rooted in Southwest Detroit community with 25+ years of trust and relationships
Holistic approach combining financial education with job training and youth development
Distributed $1,080,600 in emergency financial assistance during COVID lockdown
Culturally responsive programming in English and Spanish for Latino families
cons: Primary service area is Southwest Detroit; geographic accessibility may be limited for distant consumers
No indication of online or remote counseling services; appears to require in-person attendance
Limited information on counseling availability, appointment scheduling, or service hours on website
Website lacks specific details on financial counseling scope (credit repair vs. budgeting vs. housing focus)
No NFCC certification mentioned, though HUD housing counseling credentials are confirmed
best_for: Latino and Spanish-speaking families in Southwest Detroit needing housing or financial counseling
Parents seeking free family education and financial literacy training
Young people ages 6-24 looking for youth programs, job training, and mentorship
Adults transitioning from gang involvement or seeking workforce development and entrepreneurship support
diagnosis: DHDC is best for Southwest Detroit-area Latino families and youth needing free, culturally-responsive financial literacy, housing counseling, and education services. Primary caveat: This is a youth and family-focused community development organization rather than a specialized financial counseling agency, and services are primarily available in-person in one location.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
